system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1b: Connection bend to outlet
    hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 5 1)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 8 9 6)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (7 3 11 8)
            (6 2 10 9)

            // Block 1b walls
            (11 12 13 10)
            (3 15 14 2)
            (11 3 15 12)
            (10 2 14 13)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 16 17 13)
            (15 19 18 14)
            (12 16 19 15)
            (13 17 18 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon|omega)" 1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` â€” Block 0 is "inside-out"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

**Diagnosis:**

The first block uses vertices `(0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` which forms a degenerate/inverted hexahedron. Looking at the vertex coordinates:

- Vertex 0: (-50, 25, 25)
- Vertex 4: (0, 25, 25)
- Vertex 5: (0, 25, -25)
- Vertex 1: (-50, 25, -25)
- Vertex 3: (-50, -25, 25)
- Vertex 7: (0, -25, 25)
- Vertex 6: (0, -25, -25)
- Vertex 2: (-50, -25, -25)

This block represents the **inlet straight duct** from x=-50 to x=0. However, the vertex ordering creates a collapsed/degenerate hexahedron because vertices 0â†’4â†’5â†’1 all have y=25 (the top face is flat), making one face degenerate.

**Root Cause:** The vertex numbering doesn't follow the right-hand rule convention required by OpenFOAM for hex blocks. For a proper hex, opposite faces must be oriented consistently so that outward normals point away from the block interior.

**Resolution:**

Redefine the vertex ordering for Block 0 to form a valid hexahedron. The correct ordering should be:

```
hex (2 1 5 4 3 0 7 6)
```

This reverses the winding order so that each face follows the right-hand rule. Alternatively, more systematically, redefine **all vertex coordinates and block definitions** to follow the standard OpenFOAM convention where the first four vertices define one face and the second four define the opposite face, with consistent normal directions.

However, since the user requires matching the geometry of the squareBend tutorial, the better approach is to **reorder the vertices in blockMeshDict** so that:
- Face 1 (vertices 0,1,2,3) â†’ bottom face with inward-pointing normal in +y direction
- Face 2 (vertices 4,5,6,7) â†’ top face with inward-pointing normal in -y direction

The corrected block definition would be:
```
hex (3 2 1 0 7 6 5 4)
```

And correspondingly update all associated wall face definitions to match the new vertex ordering.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` â€” Cannot find "points" file

**Error Message:**
```
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh"
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` failed (Error 1 above), no mesh was generated. Therefore `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, `cells`, etc.) in `constant/polyMesh`.

**Resolution:**

Fix the `blockMeshDict` issue first (as described above), then re-run `blockMesh`. Once the mesh is successfully generated, `rhoSimpleFoam` will proceed with the simulation.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Patch Name Mismatches

After fixing the blockMeshDict, the boundary patches defined in the `0/` field files (`U`, `T`, `p`, `k`, `epsilon`, `nut`, `alphat`) use names that don't match those defined in `blockMeshDict`:

- `blockMeshDict` defines: `inlet`, `outlet`, `walls`
- `0/` files use: `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`

**Resolution:**

Either:
1. **Update `blockMeshDict`** to define patches named `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` (matching the 0/ files), OR
2. **Update all `0/` field files** to use the patch names `inlet`, `outlet`, `walls` with appropriate sub-faces for each wall segment.

Option 2 is recommended for consistency with the rest of the setup.

---

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Fix the vertex ordering of Block 0 (and associated wall faces) to eliminate the "inside-out" error.
2. **`0/T`, `0/U`, `0/p`, `0/k`, `0/epsilon`, `0/nut`, `0/alphat`**: Either rename boundary patches to match `blockMeshDict` (`inlet`, `outlet`, `walls`), or rename them to `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` and update `blockMeshDict` accordingly.
3. Re-run `blockMesh` after the fix, then run `rhoSimpleFoam`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
